scope: Mobile UI/UX audit only; no implementation, backend, deployment, AWS, or production-data changes.
evidence_limitations: The required AWS profile ledrigo-dev is not installed, so AWS inspection stopped before any resource read.
No authenticated production test account/session was available. Protected-screen findings use source inspection and tracked redacted evidence.
Browser zoom, OS text scaling, virtual keyboard geometry, offline injection, and restricted-role runtime states were not controllable in the connected browser.

figma.typography_proxy: Inter for the product system-ui stack

findings.title: Reconcile remains two compressed desktop tables on phones
findings.current_behavior: Two min-width 560px spreadsheet tables and compact toolbars require horizontal scrolling; context, amount, status, and action cannot be seen together.
findings.expected_mobile_behavior: A guided unresolved queue with one canonical financial row, a dedicated detail view, suggestion rationale, and sticky confirm/reject actions.
findings.likely_cause: desktop spreadsheet architecture reused below its viable width
findings.recommended_mobile_pattern: grouped unresolved list plus dedicated transaction detail/reconcile flow
findings.design_system_dependency: financial row, status, bottom action bar
findings.validation_required: synthetic expected, partial, unmatched, matched datasets at all target widths

findings.title: Ledger grid fragments accounting context across horizontal scroll
findings.current_behavior: Date, reference, payee, type, method, category, amount, balance, status, issue flags, and actions occupy a wide table with 28px inline controls.
findings.expected_mobile_behavior: Compact two-line financial rows show payee, signed amount, date, account/category, and labeled status; detail/edit actions move to a page.
findings.likely_cause: desktop inline-edit table is the only canonical ledger surface
findings.recommended_mobile_pattern: date-grouped compact financial rows plus entry detail page
findings.design_system_dependency: financial row, amount, disclosure, selection mode
findings.validation_required: long names, large/negative/zero values, deleted/matched/issue states

findings.title: Vendor detail uses five 560–1150px tables
findings.current_behavior: Bills, payments, uploads, and allocation pickers are wide fixed tables with actions at the far edge.
findings.expected_mobile_behavior: Summary header plus separate Bills, Payments, and Files grouped lists; allocation becomes a review flow.
findings.likely_cause: desktop vendor workspace compressed into one route
findings.recommended_mobile_pattern: segmented detail page with compact bill/payment rows
findings.design_system_dependency: bill row, payment row, detail header
findings.validation_required: open/partial/paid/voided bills and unapplied credit

findings.title: Complex workflows are placed in generic bottom-sheet dialogs
findings.workflow: edit, match, connect, allocate, destructive confirmation
findings.current_behavior: The same bottom-attached dialog pattern hosts short confirmations and multi-section flows; body horizontal overflow is hidden on mobile and 52+ dialog instances depend on it.
findings.expected_mobile_behavior: Short choices use sheets, serious consequences use confirmation dialogs, and complex edit/match/connect flows use full-screen routes.
findings.evidence: components/primitives/AppDialog.tsx:51-115 and static count of 52 AppDialog instances
findings.likely_cause: one overlay primitive is serving incompatible complexity levels
findings.validation_required: keyboard, browser back, focus return, unsaved changes

findings.title: Financial direction and state lose priority in dense rows
findings.current_behavior: Amount, account, source, posted/expected/matched state, and balance effect compete as columns or truncated secondary text.
findings.expected_mobile_behavior: Signed amount and direction lead visually; status uses icon plus label; account/source/date remain visible without expansion.
findings.evidence: reconcile mobile evidence and table column inventories
findings.likely_cause: column parity favored over mobile information hierarchy
findings.validation_required: negative, refund, transfer, pending, partial, stale

findings.title: Two competing mobile navigation systems create inconsistent destinations
findings.current_behavior: The main shell exposes Home/Ledger/Reconcile/Issues/More while /mobile routes expose Home/Review/Receipt/Vendors/Invoice and suppress normal chrome.
findings.expected_mobile_behavior: One stable mobile IA with role-aware top-level destinations and contextual create actions.
findings.likely_cause: task-capture mobile routes evolved separately from responsive desktop routes
findings.recommended_mobile_pattern: single four-destination bottom navigation plus More
findings.validation_required: deep links, back, business/account context, role visibility

findings.title: Core records lack a canonical mobile detail route
findings.current_behavior: Details and edits remain inline or in dialogs, making list-position return, shareable deep links, history, and progressive disclosure inconsistent.
findings.expected_mobile_behavior: A dedicated detail page preserves list filters/scroll and exposes actions, linked records, notes, and history.
findings.evidence: route inventory contains no entry or transaction detail routes
findings.likely_cause: desktop master-detail behavior lives in monolithic pages
findings.backend_dependency: possibly a record-detail endpoint if list payload is insufficient
findings.validation_required: browser back and preserved query/scroll state

findings.title: Plaid lifecycle actions are spread across similar dialogs
findings.current_behavior: Four Plaid dialogs plus account dialogs present related connect/link/reconnect/disconnect decisions without a single mobile lifecycle model.
findings.expected_mobile_behavior: Action-required account card leads to a named flow that clearly distinguishes institution, source account, local account, and consequence.
findings.likely_cause: incremental account-repair workflows share generic overlays
findings.recommended_mobile_pattern: full-screen connect/reconnect flow with review step
findings.validation_required: multi-account item, update mode, delayed/error/disconnected/archived

findings.title: Filters and summaries consume the first mobile viewport
findings.current_behavior: Stacked date fields, search, reset, counters, tabs, and source/status strips precede rows; the 390px reconcile evidence shows little record content above the fold.
findings.expected_mobile_behavior: Sticky search, compact active-filter chips, and a full-screen filter sheet with visible count/reset.
findings.likely_cause: desktop filter controls stack instead of transforming
findings.validation_required: filters persist through detail/back and show active count

findings.title: Category review is a 780px selection table on mobile
findings.current_behavior: Selection, payee, amount, category suggestions, confidence, warnings, and actions are packed into a 780px table.
findings.expected_mobile_behavior: Compact selectable rows with category and confidence disclosure; safe selection mode and sticky apply bar.
findings.likely_cause: bulk desktop workflow has no mobile selection mode
findings.design_system_dependency: selection mode and sticky action bar
findings.validation_required: select all, partial selection, long suggestions

findings.title: Accounts settings table requires up to 1180px
findings.current_behavior: Name, type, institution, last4, opening balance, status, Plaid, and actions are table columns.
findings.expected_mobile_behavior: Account cards prioritize balance/source/freshness/status with clearly named management actions.
findings.likely_cause: account administration is desktop-first
findings.recommended_mobile_pattern: account summary card plus detail flow
findings.validation_required: cash, bank, credit card, archived, reconnect states

findings.title: Upload review tables expand to 1260px
findings.current_behavior: Invoice review has 1260px minimum width, receipt 980px, and generic upload 560px.
findings.expected_mobile_behavior: File summary cards with parse state, extracted facts, preview, and a dedicated review page.
findings.likely_cause: desktop batch review presented in a dialog
findings.validation_required: parsing, duplicate, failure, long filename, preview unavailable

findings.title: Reports preserve 640px detail grids and chart density
findings.current_behavior: Detail rows retain 640px minimum width and multiple chart panels stack with desktop labels.
findings.expected_mobile_behavior: Summary-first statements with one metric per row, accessible chart summary, and drill-down detail.
findings.likely_cause: chart/table panels resize without mobile content reprioritization
findings.recommended_mobile_pattern: statement rows and accessible chart summaries
findings.validation_required: single period, many periods, negative net, no data

findings.title: Planning edits remain embedded in wide tables
findings.current_behavior: Four/five-column tables include 120px numeric inputs and row-level editing.
findings.expected_mobile_behavior: Summary list opens a focused create/edit form with review before save where needed.
findings.likely_cause: desktop spreadsheet editing reused on phones
findings.validation_required: keyboard, decimal input, validation, unsaved changes

findings.title: Core desktop controls are 28–36px and rely on coarse-pointer overrides
findings.current_behavior: Canonical inputs are h-7 and many icon buttons are h-6/h-7; a global pointer:coarse rule raises button/link minimums but not all form controls and not hybrid-device/fine-pointer mobile emulation.
findings.expected_mobile_behavior: Mobile variants have an explicit 44px minimum independent of pointer media queries.
findings.likely_cause: desktop density token reused with a partial global patch
findings.validation_required: coarse/fine pointer and 200% zoom

findings.title: Tooltip/title-only explanations have no reliable touch equivalent
findings.current_behavior: Numerous icon-only actions use title text for consequence/context; browser title tooltips are not reliably exposed by touch.
findings.expected_mobile_behavior: Visible labels in action sheets and accessible descriptions in detail flows.
findings.evidence: source-wide title attributes on financial actions
findings.likely_cause: desktop hover affordance reused on touch
findings.related_figma_frame: 13 Dialogs and Bottom Sheets / Quick Actions
findings.validation_required: screen reader and touch-only

findings.title: Wide tables lack equivalent mobile semantics
findings.current_behavior: Tables remain semantic tables, but horizontal visual reading order and sticky action columns do not provide a concise record summary or alternative view.
findings.expected_mobile_behavior: Each record exposes one labeled summary with predictable reading order and access to all fields.
findings.evidence: 20+ table surfaces and no mobile row alternatives
findings.likely_cause: visual responsive handling stopped at overflow
findings.recommended_mobile_pattern: semantic list/article rows with definition-list detail
findings.validation_required: VoiceOver/TalkBack manual review

findings.title: Dense financial layouts have high text-scaling reflow risk
findings.current_behavior: 11–12px headers, truncation, fixed column widths, five-item nav labels, and compact chips leave little reflow capacity.
findings.expected_mobile_behavior: Content reflows to 200% without loss, clipping, or two-dimensional scrolling.
findings.evidence: spreadsheet CSS and fixed table minima; runtime text scaling not controllable
findings.likely_cause: density and fixed geometry
findings.validation_required: real OS text scaling and browser zoom

findings.title: Dashboard still stacks desktop information rather than prioritizing mobile attention
findings.current_behavior: A large 1894-line dashboard combines metrics, charts, onboarding, activity, and AI; responsive wrapping does not establish a single attention-first order.
findings.expected_mobile_behavior: Urgent work, balances/freshness, upcoming/overdue, recent activity, then optional analytics.
findings.likely_cause: desktop card set stacked on small screens
findings.validation_required: empty, healthy, urgent, stale, many accounts

findings.title: Complex form keyboard and unsaved-change behavior is unverified
findings.current_behavior: Forms live in scrollable dialogs or inline tables; there is no shared scroll-to-error or unsaved-change guard visible in primitives.
findings.expected_mobile_behavior: Keyboard-safe full-screen forms with sticky submit, visible labels, error summary, and back protection.
findings.evidence: AppDialog primitive and route source; virtual keyboard not available in audit browser
findings.likely_cause: desktop dialog form architecture
findings.design_system_dependency: form controls and bottom action bar
findings.validation_required: real mobile keyboard and back navigation

findings.title: Public landing is usable but excessively long on small phones
findings.current_behavior: No horizontal overflow, but the page is approximately 5,029px tall at 320px and repeats the same step description.
findings.expected_mobile_behavior: Shorter proof-driven story with a persistent, clear sign-in/create path.
findings.likely_cause: desktop marketing sections stack fully
findings.validation_required: content review

findings.title: Authentication form is a strong mobile pattern to preserve
findings.current_behavior: One semantic form uses visible labels, 16px inputs, 44px controls, a skip link, and no horizontal overflow.
findings.expected_mobile_behavior: Preserve this pattern and reuse its form sizing/label behavior.
findings.evidence: production browser DOM and measurements 2026-07-15; prod-login-320x568.png
findings.likely_cause: purpose-built authentication layout
findings.validation_required: keyboard and error announcement

findings.title: Reconcile virtualization and keep-last-good behavior are strengths
findings.current_behavior: Large reconcile lists use TanStack virtualization and refresh paths preserve last-good data.
findings.expected_mobile_behavior: Retain these data/performance behaviors behind the new mobile presentation.
findings.likely_cause: deliberate performance remediation
findings.validation_required: screen-reader review with virtualization

findings.title: Header utility buttons are 32px in fine-pointer mobile contexts
findings.current_behavior: Menu, activity, and account buttons use h-8/w-8; pointer:coarse increases minimum size only when the media query matches.
findings.expected_mobile_behavior: Mobile header controls are explicitly 44x44.
findings.likely_cause: desktop topbar density
findings.validation_required: hybrid pointer devices

findings.title: Local protected-route E2E check depends on undocumented API environment
findings.current_behavior: The protected-route test fails before the auth guard because NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL is absent; seven public tests pass and one is skipped.
findings.expected_mobile_behavior: A documented safe test environment permits protected-route and mobile-state validation without production credentials.
findings.evidence: npm run test:e2e on 2026-07-15: 7 passed, 2 failed, 1 skipped
findings.likely_cause: missing local env contract
findings.recommended_mobile_pattern: synthetic authenticated fixture and deterministic API stub
findings.validation_required: rerun with approved synthetic environment
